english: to Sch. also an interjection expressive of pain (?).
a Chinese word, shoe C.
n. of a medicinal herb Med.
Lex. sugar. treacle Cs., hágs-kyi (hags kyi) lú-tu (lu tu).
haṅ? (hang) haṅ-dzom-pa (hang dzom pa) to squander, to dissipate Sch., haṅ-ča-byed (hang ca byed) a squanderer Sch.
W. *háṅ-če* (hang ce) to pant, to gasp.
suddenly Sch. — had-po? (had po) Mil.
or hur-húr (hur hur), with *čó-če* (co ce) to exert one's self, to strive W.
W. 1. dumb, mute, *han-dáṅ-ṅi (han dang ngi) (s)péra* ((s)pe ra) a stammering, also: a confused, unmeaning speech. — 2. imbecile, weak of mind.
v. ha-na-ho-né (ha na ho ne).

1. a mouthful, háb-za byéd-pa (hab za byed pa), hab-háb zá-ba (hab hab za ba) to devour greedily, e.g. of dogs, pigs etc., hab-bčád (hab bcad) a needy wretch, a starveling, famishing person. — 2. a stitch, in sewing, also *háb-ka* (hab ka) C. *háb-so* (hab so) W., hab-tsem-pa (hab tsem pa) to make here a stitch and there a stitch, as in quilting Mil. nt. — 3. hab-gód (hab god) v. ha (ha) 3. — 4. v. the following article.
a dispute, a quarrel, háb-śa byéd-pa (hab sha byed pa) Mil., Thgr, to dispute, to quarrel; hab-tób byéd pa (hab tob byed pa) to scramble for, to strive or contend for Pth.

1. avarice, covetousness, greediness; W. also vb.: to covet, *żaṅ-ni nór-la* (zhang ni nor la) after a person's wealth; to long for, to yearn after, *k'oï 'á-ne hám-te dad dug* (kho'i 'a ne ham te dad dug) his wife sits yearning (after him); hám-pa byéd-pa (ham pa byed pa) Sch.: to be covetous. — 2. strength Cs.; courage, bravery W., of men and animals. — 3. white film on liquids etc., mould C., *hám-ḍi* (ham di) or *-ri* (-ri) a musty, fusty smell C., W.; *hám-por čág-k'an* (ham por cag khan) W mouldy, musty, *hám-če* (ham ce) W. to get mouldy. — 4. lie, falsehood, C. — hám-pa-čan (ham pa can) 1. covetous, avaricious; greedy, voracious. 2. courageous: one Lex. explains rlam-kyér (rlam kyer) by hám-pas kyér-ba(?) (ham pas kyer ba); *hám-pa čuṅ-se* (ham pa cung se) W. cowardly.
W. rime, hoar-frost?

suddenly, har láṅ-ba (har lang ba) to rise suddenly Mil.; *har se jhé-pa* (har se jhe pa) to rouse suddenly from sleep C.; har-gyis (har gyis) (Sch. had-kyis (had kyis)) more precise form of the adv. Tar.
empty, open, Tar. 115, 16 Schf.
a porridge, made of milk, butter and honey.
to pant, to wheeze. to snort, hal-kyí (hal kyi) a panting dog Sch.; *hal-méd* (hal med) W. weak.
exaggeration, hyperbole, has - čer smrá-ba (has cer smra ba) Cs, *hẹ gyab-če* (he gyab ce) W. to exaggerate, to talk big, to brag.
n. of a mountain in C., Glr.
numeral: 59.
or hig-dig (hig dig), W. *zér-če* (zer ce) to blow one's nose.
Ssk., = gaṅs-čan (gangs can) the snowy mountains, Himalaya.
Ssk. diamond.
corn-stack, *hi-ri gyáb-če* (hi ri gyab ce) W. to pile up a stack of corn.
noise, *hi-liṅ táṅ-k'an* (hi ling tang khan) W., bully, brawler.
= ha-há (ha ha).
Sch. breast-bone.

the act of sobbing. *hig táṅ-te dug* (hig tang te dug) or *gyáb-te dug* (gyab te dug) he is sobbing W.; *hig jáṅ* (hig jang) (lit. sbyaṅ (sbyang)) *dug* (dug) is said to be an expression used of a Lama, when he is watching the gradual departing of the soul of a dying man.
= śiṅ-kun (shing kun), Asa foetida.
C.: *hiṅ-du-tá-ni kẹ* (hing du ta ni ke), Hindoostanee, the language of the Hindoos.
Pur.: *hiz yoṅ* (hiz yong) he is panting.
1. W. breath, *hu gyáb-če* (hu gyab ce) to breathe. — 2. num.: 89.
